duplicateCheck.details: master-document/1.5_Session_In_Progress_Flow.md and 1.6_End_Session_Flow.md contain narrative descriptions of this phase. No Feature SDS exists for session-in-progress yet. The booking-and-allocation Feature SDS covers booking lifecycle but stops before ACTIVE state behavior. This SDS covers everything from ACTIVE → COMPLETED.

rawRequestSummary: Create a Feature SDS for the Session In-Progress phase, covering the UI, APIs, schedulers, schema changes, and deferred items that govern the experience from booking status ACTIVE through COMPLETED.
approvedRequirement: When a booking transitions to ACTIVE, both client and companions enter the session-in-progress screen. The client sees a countdown timer (time remaining), static companion info (names/photos, no live map), and can extend the session once (+1 hour), end the session early, or trigger SOS. Companions see a countdown timer, their designation, and have access to a Captain↔ViceCaptain-only HTTP-polled chat. A server-side scheduler auto-ends sessions at endAt and sends a 15-min push+in-app notification before session end. A separate scheduler checks companion GPS against venue coordinates every ~60s and alerts admin + companion if breach > 300m. SOS API is a stub for now (returns 200, no side effects). Schema requires two additions: nearEndNotifiedAt on Booking, and a new BookingMessage table.
businessGoal: Define the complete in-progress session experience so it can be built as a coherent feature: timer-driven UI, session controls (extend/end/SOS), companion chat, automated session management jobs, and location breach monitoring.

owningFeature.name: session-in-progress
owningFeature.reason: This is a net-new feature SDS. No existing Feature SDS owns the ACTIVE booking state behavior.

businessRules: Session timer is a countdown from total duration (2h standard, 3h if extended). Updates every second on client.
Extend: adds exactly +1 hour to Booking.endAt. Sets Booking.extendedAt. Guard: status=ACTIVE, extendedAt IS NULL, caller role=CLIENT. Only one extension per booking.
End session (client): calls existing POST /bookings/:id/cancel. Works on ACTIVE status. Requires confirmation modal. Redirects to rating page.
SOS: POST /bookings/:id/sos. Stub — returns 200 with no side effects. Full implementation is deferred.

15-min notification: fires once when endAt - NOW() <= 15 min AND nearEndNotifiedAt IS NULL AND status=ACTIVE. Sets nearEndNotifiedAt on fire. Delivers push notification to device AND in-app banner if app is foregrounded.
Session auto-end: scheduler checks ACTIVE bookings where endAt <= NOW(). Transitions Booking status ACTIVE → COMPLETED atomically. Redirects both client and companions to rating page.

300m monitoring: server job runs every ~60s for ACTIVE bookings. Reads last known GPS from BookingParticipantLocation for each companion. Compares to Venue.latitude/longitude using Haversine formula. If > 300m: record breach, alert admin, send push notification to that companion. Client is NOT notified.
Companion chat: Captain and Vice Captain only. Client cannot read or send. Scoped strictly to bookingId.
Companion chat polling: app polls GET /bookings/:id/messages every 3-5 seconds while in-progress screen is active.
Companions cannot extend or end the session. Only the client has those controls.
Companion sees their designation (Captain / Vice Captain) on in-progress screen.
Client sees static companion info (name, photo) — no live map, no GPS pins during in-progress phase.
Notification tapping Yes triggers extend flow. Tapping No is a no-op.
stateTransitions: Booking: ACTIVE → COMPLETED (auto-end scheduler or time expiry)
Booking: ACTIVE → CANCELLED (client ends session early via existing cancel API)
Booking.extendedAt: NULL → timestamp (on extend, once only)
Booking.nearEndNotifiedAt: NULL → timestamp (on 15-min notification, once only)
validations: Extend: reject if extendedAt IS NOT NULL
Extend: reject if caller is not the booking's clientId
Chat send: reject if caller is not CAPTAIN or VICE_CAPTAIN assigned to this booking
Chat read: reject if caller is not CAPTAIN or VICE_CAPTAIN assigned to this booking
SOS: reject if caller is not the client or an assigned companion of the booking
300m job: skip check if no GPS entry exists for a companion (treat as no breach)
dependencies: booking-and-allocation Feature SDS (Booking lifecycle, cancel API already built)
matching Feature SDS (BookingParticipantLocation table, location upsert endpoint — reused as-is)
Haversine formula already implemented in matching.service.ts
POST /bookings/:id/cancel already built and handles ACTIVE status
POST /bookings/:id/matching/location already built — reused for in-progress GPS posting
nonGoals: SOS full implementation (admin alert, emergency contact display, in-app support chat) — deferred
Real-time WebSocket/SSE chat — HTTP polling only for MVP
Client-visible live map during in-progress — removed
Rating page implementation — covered in 1.6, separate feature SDS scope
Push notification infrastructure design — assumed available, implementation detail left to infra

newApis.description: Extend session by +1 hour. Guards: ACTIVE, extendedAt IS NULL, caller=CLIENT
SOS stub. Returns 200. No side effects. Full impl deferred.
Retrieve chat messages for booking. Companion-only.
Send a chat message. Companion-only. Scoped to booking.
Retrieve session timing/status for in-progress countdown (client + assigned companions).
Reused as-is from matching phase for in-progress GPS posting.
Reused as-is. Handles ACTIVE → CANCELLED for client end-session.

newSchedulers.name: session-auto-end
newSchedulers.action: Transition ACTIVE → COMPLETED. Trigger rating page redirect for both parties.

newSchedulers.name: near-end-notification
newSchedulers.trigger: endAt - NOW() <= 15 min AND nearEndNotifiedAt IS NULL AND status=ACTIVE
newSchedulers.action: Send push + in-app notification. Set nearEndNotifiedAt.

newSchedulers.name: companion-300m-breach-check
newSchedulers.action: Read last GPS from BookingParticipantLocation. Haversine check vs Venue. If > 300m: alert admin + push notify companion.

schemaChanges.change: Add nearEndNotifiedAt (nullable DateTime). Tracks whether 15-min notification has already fired.

schemaChanges.constraints: bookingId FK → Booking. senderUserId FK → User. No soft delete needed.

versioningHint.reason: Net-new Feature SDS. No prior version exists. New APIs, new schedulers, new schema entities.

acceptanceCriteria: Client sees countdown timer from booking duration. Timer adjusts immediately on extend.
Client can extend session once. Second attempt is rejected.
Client can end session early. Confirmation modal shown. Redirects to rating page.
SOS API returns 200 for valid callers. No other behavior.
Captain and Vice Captain can send and retrieve messages scoped to their booking. Client cannot.
Session auto-ends at endAt. Booking transitions to COMPLETED.
15-min notification fires once per booking. Does not re-fire if already sent.
300m breach detected within ~60s of companion leaving the venue radius. Admin alerted. Companion notified.
Companions cannot call extend or end-session APIs.
nearEndNotifiedAt is set on notification fire and prevents duplicate notifications.
BookingMessage records are scoped to booking and accessible only to assigned companions.
constraints: No WebSocket or SSE — HTTP polling only for chat
No live map on in-progress screen — static companion info only
SOS is a stub — no admin notification logic in this scope
Extension limit of once per booking enforced by extendedAt field (core SDS invariant)
Haversine formula already exists in codebase — reuse, do not rewrite
Push notification infrastructure assumed available — SDS should not define the infra layer
featureSdsAgentInstructions: Create a new Feature SDS file: session-in-progress.feature-sds.md
Reference booking-and-allocation.feature-sds.md for booking lifecycle context. Do not duplicate its content.
Mark SOS as a deferred side note in the SDS — stub API defined, full behavior out of scope.
Include both client and companion views as separate sections.
Capture all three schedulers with trigger conditions and actions.
Capture schema changes (nearEndNotifiedAt, BookingMessage) with field definitions.
Do not invent any business rules not listed in this artifact.
Stop if any conflict arises with core_sds.md invariants and surface it before proceeding.
